descGu: Sprite, event, loop — real coding concepts!
descEn: Sprite, events, loops — think like a coder!

teacherGuide.conceptGu: Scratch = visual coding. Sprite = movable character (cat, robot). Event = 'when something happens' (flag click, key press). Loop = repeat. Condition = if/then. These 4 = foundation of ALL programming.
teacherGuide.conceptEn: Sprite=object, Event=trigger, Loop=repeat, Condition=if-then. These are universal programming concepts.
teacherGuide.storyGu: 'Scratch cat = sprite. Click green flag = event! Cat moves 10 steps = action. Repeat 10 times = loop! IF touching wall THEN turn = condition! Real Python, JavaScript same ideas!'
teacherGuide.scriptGu: 'Open scratch.mit.edu! Make cat walk. Click flag — what happens? Change number — see difference! You are programming!'
teacherGuide.tipsGu: 💡 scratch.mit.edu — free, browser, no install! Make cat say 'Hello Yuval!' first. Then move. Then loop. Step by step!

questions.textEn: In Scratch, a movable character is called a ___?
questions.hintEn: Any movable object in Scratch = Sprite!

questions.textEn: Clicking the green flag in Scratch is an example of ___?
questions.hintGu: Flag click = 'When this happens, do this' = Event!
questions.hintEn: Flag click triggers the program = Event!

questions.textEn: Repeating 'Move 10 steps' 5 times is using a ___?
questions.hintGu: Repeat = Loop! UNO game loop = same turns repeat!

questions.textGu: Cat ને dance કરાવવા steps ક્રમ?
questions.textEn: Put these Scratch steps in order to make cat dance!
questions.hintEn: Event first, then loop, then actions inside!

questions.textGu: Cat wall touch કરે THEN bounce back = ___?
questions.textEn: IF cat touches wall THEN bounce — this is a ___?
questions.hintGu: IF something THEN do = Condition! AND gate ની જેમ!
questions.hintEn: IF...THEN = Condition — same as logic gates!

questions.textEn: Scratch coloured blocks represent real coding ___?
questions.hintGu: Loop in Scratch = loop in Python = loop in any language!
